novel_features.name: Find a quote across all transcripts
novel_features.description: Search every locally-recorded and cloud-recorded Zoom transcript at once, with speaker filter, context windows, and a clickable deep link back to the exact second.
novel_features.rationale: Requires FTS5 across both local VTT cues on disk and cloud recording transcripts in the local store — no Zoom tool searches both layers in one query.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When you need to recover a specific commitment or decision from a meeting, you don't know in advance whether it was recorded locally or to the cloud. One search box.

novel_features.name: Storage audit of local recordings
novel_features.description: Group everything under ~/Documents/Zoom/ by month, topic, or partial-conversion status; cross-check against the cloud recordings list to flag duplicates safe to delete locally.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the on-disk filesystem walk with the cloud_recordings table — no API call returns the local view, no local tool sees what's in the cloud.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the Documents folder is the biggest thing on the laptop, agents need to surface which gigabytes are safely reclaimable.

novel_features.name: Recording drift detector
novel_features.description: Set-difference between local and cloud recordings; flags cloud recordings approaching the org retention deadline and local double_click_to_convert partials whose cloud version is already complete.
novel_features.rationale: Requires both layers in one SQLite store; no single endpoint exposes the diff.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Cloud retention silently deletes recordings. Local partials silently fail. The agent needs to surface both before they bite.

novel_features.name: Today's meeting load + conflicts
novel_features.description: One screen: every meeting on your calendar today, every saved bookmark scheduled for today, every recording made today, and any overlapping intervals flagged as conflicts.
novel_features.rationale: UNION across cloud meetings, saved_meetings bookmarks, and local_recordings — no single endpoint composes all three.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the agent is asked 'what's on my plate today and is anything double-booked,' it needs the answer in one call.

novel_features.name: Bookmark from URL paste
novel_features.description: Paste any Zoom URL shape (https://zoom.us/j/<id>?pwd=, zoommtg://, calendar-invite formats) and the CLI extracts ID + unencrypted password into a named saved bookmark in one step.
novel_features.rationale: Regex-parses Zoom's specific URL shapes (a service-specific content pattern); the absorbed `saved add` command forces the user to dissect the URL by hand.
novel_features.why_it_matters: URLs land in Slack/email constantly. Closing the parse-it-yourself gap turns 'save this for later' into one command.

novel_features.name: Schedule + bookmark in one shot
novel_features.description: Create a cloud meeting (POST /users/me/meetings) and immediately persist the resulting ID + password into local saved_meetings, so future `zoom saved join <name>` works offline.
novel_features.rationale: Cloud write + local cache round-trip — no existing CLI or MCP does this combo.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Scheduling and joining are usually two separate tools. Pairing them lets agents create-then-recall meetings without re-querying the cloud.

novel_features.name: Speaker-time analytics on a recording
novel_features.description: Per-speaker total talk-seconds, longest monologue, and cue-overlap interruption count, computed from VTT cue timestamps and speaker labels.
novel_features.rationale: Zoom VTT cues carry speaker labels per cue (Teams/Meet don't index this way) — pure mechanical computation on a Zoom-specific content shape.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the agent is asked 'who dominated the meeting' or 'did everyone get a chance to speak,' it needs the answer without an LLM transcription pass.

novel_features.name: Export a recording bundle
novel_features.description: Resolve a recording ID against local first, fall back to cloud (downloading if needed); package mp4 + vtt + chat.txt + a generated INDEX.md with timestamped table of contents into one folder.
novel_features.rationale: One verb for local OR cloud; the INDEX.md TOC is derived from VTT cues — no existing tool produces a share-ready bundle.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the agent is asked to 'pull together everything we have on Tuesday's planning call,' it needs one verb that doesn't care whether the source is local or cloud.

novel_features.name: My Notes — open the Zoom Notes web portal
novel_features.description: Open https://zoom.us/notes (optionally scoped to a meeting) in the user's default browser — the only path to the live Notes UI since Zoom has no public REST endpoint for the My Notes feature.
novel_features.rationale: Zoom devs confirmed in 2024 and 2025 forum threads that no API for My Notes exists; opening the web portal is the canonical workaround.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to send the user to their notes, this is the single command that always works regardless of auth state.

novel_features.name: My Notes — AI Companion meeting summary
novel_features.description: Fetches Zoom AI Companion's auto-generated meeting summary for a meeting UUID via the documented `/meetings/{uuid}/meeting_summary` endpoint (S2S OAuth gated).
novel_features.rationale: Closest documented API to 'what was said and decided' content the My Notes UI shows; uses the public AI Companion endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs the canonical post-meeting recap without manually exporting a PDF.

novel_features.name: My Notes — AI Companion transcript
novel_features.description: Fetches the AI Companion full transcript for a meeting UUID via the documented `/meetings/{uuid}/transcript` endpoint (S2S OAuth gated).
novel_features.rationale: Companion to `notes summary`; provides verbatim transcript when the summary isn't enough.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents that need to verify a summary or quote can pull verbatim transcript without opening the web portal.

novel_features.name: My Notes — ingest exported PDF or DOCX
novel_features.description: Parses a Notes PDF or DOCX exported from the Zoom web portal, extracts text + meeting metadata + headings, indexes them in a local SQLite `notes` table (FTS5 enabled).
novel_features.rationale: The only way to get raw My Notes text on disk today is the manual PDF/DOCX export from zoom.us/notes; once on disk, the CLI owns the data forever.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets agents build a searchable, persistent corpus of the user's meeting notes from manual exports.

novel_features.name: My Notes — search across ingested notes
novel_features.description: FTS5 query across every Notes file that has been ingested, returns `meeting_topic + note_excerpt + source_file + match_offset` with optional `--since` / `--meeting-id` filters.
novel_features.rationale: Once ingested, the user's My Notes become greppable across every meeting — a capability the web portal does not provide.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents asked 'what did I write down about X' can answer instantly from the ingested corpus.

novel_features.name: My Notes — extract to-do / action items
novel_features.description: Scans ingested notes for action-item patterns (`TODO:`, `Action:`, `[ ]`, `- [ ]`, `Action Item:`, `Next:`, `Follow up:`, `Owner:`) and emits a structured to-do list with source meeting topic + date + checkbox state.
novel_features.rationale: Directly serves the user's stated weekly ritual: 'I build my own to-do lists from them.' No existing tool extracts action items from Zoom Notes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Turns the My Notes archive into a queryable backlog — the killer feature for agents that need to surface 'what do I still owe people from last week's meetings.'

narrative.headline: The first Zoom CLI that joins your local desktop app, your on-disk recordings, and your cloud account into one agent-native surface.
narrative.value_prop: Start and join meetings via the desktop URL scheme (no browser interstitial), search every transcript you've ever recorded locally or in the cloud with one query, drive mute/unmute/video/leave from the command line on macOS, and run the full Zoom REST API (users, meetings, webinars, recordings, reports, dashboards) when your Server-to-Server OAuth credentials are configured. SQLite-backed; --json, --select, --dry-run on every command; works offline for the local surface and on-disk recordings.
narrative.auth_narrative: Local commands (start, join, mute, leave, recordings local, search) require no Zoom account — just the Zoom desktop app installed. Cloud commands (meetings, users, webinars, recordings cloud, reports, metrics) need Server-to-Server OAuth credentials. Run `zoom-pp-cli auth set-token` to provide ZOOM_S2S_ACCOUNT_ID, ZOOM_S2S_CLIENT_ID, ZOOM_S2S_CLIENT_SECRET — the CLI caches the access token and auto-refreshes on 401.

quickstart.comment: Confirms the Zoom app is installed, the URL handler is registered, ~/Documents/Zoom exists, and surfaces macOS accessibility-permission gaps before they bite.
Walks ~/Documents/Zoom/, parses every VTT transcript into a local FTS5 index — usually a few seconds.
The killer command: searches every local and cloud transcript at once with speaker filter and clickable timestamps.
Composes your cloud calendar, saved bookmarks, and today's local recordings into one view with conflict detection.
Pastes any Zoom URL, opens it directly in the desktop app — no browser interstitial. --dry-run prints the zoommtg:// URL it would launch.
macOS only — flips mute via the running Zoom app's Meeting menu. Safe no-op if not in a meeting.

quickstart.command: zoom-pp-cli notes ingest ~/Downloads/zoom-notes-2026-05-12.pdf && zoom-pp-cli notes todos --since 7d --json
quickstart.comment: The My Notes pipeline: drop in an exported PDF/DOCX from zoom.us/notes, then extract action items across the last week.

troubleshoots.symptom: `open: zoommtg://...: No application knows how to open URL`
troubleshoots.fix: Run `zoom-pp-cli doctor` — it tells you whether the URL handler is registered. Reinstalling the Zoom desktop app re-registers it.

troubleshoots.symptom: `mute/unmute` returns `osascript: not supported on this platform`
troubleshoots.fix: These commands are macOS-only. On Linux/Windows, use the Zoom client's keyboard shortcuts directly.
Grant accessibility permission to your terminal: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility → add Terminal/iTerm. `zoom-pp-cli doctor` checks this.

troubleshoots.symptom: Cloud commands return `auth: ZOOM_S2S_* not set`
troubleshoots.fix: Run `zoom-pp-cli auth set-token` or export `ZOOM_S2S_ACCOUNT_ID`, `ZOOM_S2S_CLIENT_ID`, `ZOOM_S2S_CLIENT_SECRET`. Get them from your Zoom App Marketplace Server-to-Server OAuth app.
The CLI honors `Retry-After`; if you're hitting daily caps, narrow `--from`/`--to` windows or use the local cache (`--data-source local`).
Skipped files are usually `double_click_to_convert` partials. Run `zoom-pp-cli recordings local list --partial-only` to see them; double-click in Finder to trigger Zoom's conversion.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent needs to reach across the user's Zoom surface holistically — joining the right meeting now, surfacing what's on the calendar today, finding what was said in a past meeting, or managing the cloud account. Particularly powerful when local desktop recordings and cloud-recorded meetings need to be queried together. Not the right pick for embedding live Zoom video into a custom app (use the Meeting SDK) or for hosting bot participants (use Recall.ai or the Meeting SDK).

recipes.title: Find a quote from any past meeting
recipes.explanation: Searches every local + cloud transcript for the phrase, scoped to one speaker, with 45 seconds of follow-up context per match — and returns a clickable timestamped deep link.

recipes.title: Triage Monday morning
recipes.explanation: Pulls the last week of meetings + today's calendar + today's recordings in one query, flags any back-to-back overlaps, and gives the agent everything it needs to draft a weekly recap.

recipes.title: Clean up Documents/Zoom safely
recipes.explanation: Groups local recordings by month and flags which are also in the cloud (and therefore safe to delete locally) — agents can recommend reclaimable disk without risking the only copy.

recipes.title: Schedule a meeting and stash it
recipes.explanation: Creates the cloud meeting and immediately writes a local bookmark, so a later `zoom-pp-cli saved join sprint` works without re-hitting the API.

recipes.title: Audit speaker time on yesterday's offsite
recipes.explanation: Computes per-speaker talk-time, longest monologue, and interruption count from the VTT cues without an LLM call.

recipes.title: Build a to-do list from your meeting notes
recipes.command: zoom-pp-cli notes ingest ~/Downloads/notes-q2-planning.pdf && zoom-pp-cli notes todos --since 14d --json --select text,meeting_topic,owner,checked
recipes.explanation: Ingest an exported Notes PDF, then extract every action-item pattern across the last two weeks of ingested notes — Zoom has no public API for My Notes, so manual export plus this pipeline is the only path.

trigger_phrases: what zoom meetings do i have today
find that quote from last week's zoom
extract todos from my zoom notes
